description: Theoretical Investigation: Why does a₀ exist?

facts.statement: An acceleration scale a₀ ≈ 1.2×10⁻¹⁰ m/s² appears in galaxy dynamics
facts.evidence: RAR, BTFR, rotation curves of 175 SPARC + 22 LITTLE THINGS galaxies
facts.robustness: Survives 4 breaking tests (null, scramble, M/L, distance)

facts.statement: a₀ is approximately universal across galaxy types
facts.evidence: Per-galaxy scatter 0.33-0.57 dex (observational), no systematic mass/size dependence (partial r|Vmax = -0.656)
facts.caveat: Scatter is large enough to hide weak dependencies
Could be numerological coincidence (not yet ruled out)

facts.statement: The relationship g_obs = f(g_bar) is tight with minimal scatter
facts.evidence: RAR scatter < 0.13 dex (McGaugh+2016), our analysis confirms
facts.implication: Dark matter "knows" where baryons are — or gravity itself changes

facts.statement: a₀ works for galaxies but NOT fully for clusters
facts.evidence: Floor reduces Newton deficit by ~43% in clusters, ~52.5% residual remains
facts.implication: Any explanation must account for cluster failure or predict additional physics

conditions.condition: DERIVE a₀ as a scale (not insert it by hand)
conditions.test: Does the theory predict a₀ without being told about it?
Does the derived a₀ depend on galaxy properties?
Does cosmology naturally enter the local dynamics?

conditions.condition: Recover Newtonian limit at high acceleration
conditions.test: Does g → g_bar when g_bar >> a₀?
Why does a₀ underperform in clusters?

conditions.condition: Have a mechanism (not just a formula)
conditions.test: Is there a field equation, action, or physical process?

deSitter.verdict: Within factor ~0.6, but missing 2π and too small

hubble.verdict: Best match (ratio 0.87), but 2π needs physical justification

unruh.verdict: Provides a REASON (minimum temperature), but gives same number as de Sitter

mainQuestion: Why does cH₀ appear as a local acceleration in galaxies?
mostPromisingPaths: Cluster failure as constraint on mechanism
